full_text:
Thunderstorms continue across Rajasthan, yellow alert in 11 districts:Jaipur records death, orange alert for 7 districts on May 4
- Copy link
Rajasthan continues to witness thunderstorms and rain today. Strong winds have been blowing since morning in Jaipur and nearby areas. Clouds are thundering and light rainfall is also being recorded. The Meteorological Centre has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain in 11 districts today as well.
On Saturday too, several districts including Jaipur, Sikar, Jhunjhunu, Alwar, Bikaner and Churu witnessed dust storms accompanied by rain. One person died in stormy rain in Jaipur. Hundreds of trees were uprooted. Several houses in different areas were also damaged.
Weather experts have predicted that this spell of thunderstorms and rain will continue for the next three days across the state. An orange alert has also been issued for 7 districts on May 4, warning of strong winds and storms.
In the last 24 hours, areas of Jaisalmer, Phalodi and Barmer experienced intense heat, with temperatures recorded at 44°C.
Pictures of weather in Rajasthan...
These cities remained the hottest
Wind speed of 80 kmph in Jaipur: Jaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 43.5°C this season so far. The temperature has dropped by more than 5°C over the past five days. The impact of the western disturbance continued on Saturday as well. After light morning rain, a storm and rainfall in the evening brought major relief from heat. Winds blew at speeds of up to 80 kmph during the storm.
Storm with rain: Weather changed across Bikaner, Churu, Phalodi, Sikar, Ajmer and nearby areas on Saturday. Dense clouds formed, strong dusty winds blew and light rain was recorded in some districts. Earlier in the day, these cities had clear skies and temperatures above 40°C.
Temperature above 44°C, intense heat: While some districts were affected by the western disturbance on Saturday, others continued to face strong sunshine and heat. The hottest regions were Jaisalmer, Phalodi and Barmer. Maximum temperatures recorded were 44.4°C in Jaisalmer and Phalodi, and 44.3°C in Barmer.
What next: According to the Meteorological Department, the western disturbance impact will be strongest on May 4. Winds may reach speeds of up to 70 kmph in Jaipur, Bikaner, Bharatpur and Ajmer divisions. Relief from heatwave conditions is expected for the next one week.
See ahead the condition of cities
One death in Jaipur due to storm
In Jaipur, trees and electricity lines were damaged in several areas due to storm and rain on Saturday evening. Heavy rainfall continued for about one and a half hours, leading to waterlogging in several places. Temples and houses were also damaged. One young man died due to the storm. Earlier in the morning too, Jaipur had witnessed rainfall. The weather change brought relief from heat.
A high-tension electricity wire broke in Jaipur, and a young man came into contact with it and died due to electrocution.
Slight temperature drop in Udaipur
Heat conditions continue in Udaipur. On Saturday, maximum temperature saw a slight drop, but daytime heat remained similar to previous days. On May 2, Udaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 40.8°C and minimum of 26.0°C, slightly lower than May 1, when it was 41.1°C and 26.5°C.
Strong storm and rain in Sikar
Sikar recorded a maximum temperature of 40.5°C and minimum of 25.2°C on Saturday. Weather changed in the evening, with dense clouds appearing after 6 pm. Strong winds of 50–60 kmph accompanied by light rain were recorded.
Intense heat in Kota
Kota continued to experience heat on Saturday. Maximum temperature was recorded at 39°C and minimum at 28.8°C. Strong sunshine and humidity made afternoons uncomfortable for people. However, light rain occurred in the evening.
Heat effect continued in Ajmer
Ajmer continued to experience hot weather on Saturday. Temperature dropped compared to Friday. The Meteorological Department recorded a maximum of 38.7°C and minimum of 22.2°C. On Friday, it was 41.1°C and 24.0°C. Strong sunshine persisted in the morning, but winds provided slight relief.
Alwar had clouds and sunshine alternately
Ajmer continued to experience hot weather on Saturday. Temperature dropped compared to Friday. The Meteorological Department recorded a maximum of 38.7°C and minimum of 22.2°C. On Friday, it was 41.1°C and 24.0°C. Strong sunshine persisted in the morning, but winds provided slight relief.
Tourists decreased in Jodhpur due to heat
Heat conditions are increasing in Jodhpur. On Saturday, maximum temperature reached 42.6°C. Due to rising heat, tourist numbers at major attractions are declining. At Macheeya Safari Park, visitor numbers have reduced by almost half.
